selected_problem_solved:
The proof establishes a computable countable structure \(\mathcal A\) in a finite relational language with universe \(\omega\), whose automorphism group is exactly
\[
\{\alpha_F:F\subseteq\omega\text{ finite}\},
\]
where \(\alpha_F\) toggles the \(U\)-sort by symmetric difference with \(F\) and swaps the two \(V\)-rays over precisely the labels in \(F\).

It further establishes that for every copy \(\mathcal B\cong\mathcal A\) and every automorphism \(\rho\in\operatorname{Aut}(\mathcal B)\), one has
\[
\rho\leq_T D(\mathcal B),
\]
nonuniformly in \(\rho\). Hence \(\mathcal A\) is computably \(\operatorname{Aut}\)-countable on a cone, with cone base \(C=\emptyset\).

Finally, for every finite parameter tuple \(\bar a\), the proof chooses a label \(l\) whose \(V\)-gadget contains no element of \(\bar a\), sets \(\pi=\alpha_{\{l\}}\), and proves that \(\operatorname{Graph}(\pi)\) is not definable over
\[
\operatorname{rng}(\bar a)\cup \pi[\operatorname{rng}(\bar a)]
\]
by any countable disjunction of existential formulas of the stated \(\Sigma^{in}_1\) form. The key argument is the strong self-embedding shifting one ray of the \(l\)-gadget, which transfers every existential truth from the true graph pair \((r_0,r_1)\) to the false pair \((s_0,r_1)\).
selector_skipped_reason: N_PARALLEL_RUNS=1 — single run, no selection needed

all_runs.label: A
all_runs.status: Track A finalize complete (full-proof polish, seed=verified_full_proof). See solution.tex and benchmark.json.
all_runs.problem_solved:
The proof establishes a computable countable structure \(\mathcal A\) in a finite relational language with universe \(\omega\), whose automorphism group is exactly
\[
\{\alpha_F:F\subseteq\omega\text{ finite}\},
\]
where \(\alpha_F\) toggles the \(U\)-sort by symmetric difference with \(F\) and swaps the two \(V\)-rays over precisely the labels in \(F\).

It further establishes that for every copy \(\mathcal B\cong\mathcal A\) and every automorphism \(\rho\in\operatorname{Aut}(\mathcal B)\), one has
\[
\rho\leq_T D(\mathcal B),
\]
nonuniformly in \(\rho\). Hence \(\mathcal A\) is computably \(\operatorname{Aut}\)-countable on a cone, with cone base \(C=\emptyset\).

Finally, for every finite parameter tuple \(\bar a\), the proof chooses a label \(l\) whose \(V\)-gadget contains no element of \(\bar a\), sets \(\pi=\alpha_{\{l\}}\), and proves that \(\operatorname{Graph}(\pi)\) is not definable over
\[
\operatorname{rng}(\bar a)\cup \pi[\operatorname{rng}(\bar a)]
\]
by any countable disjunction of existential formulas of the stated \(\Sigma^{in}_1\) form. The key argument is the strong self-embedding shifting one ray of the \(l\)-gadget, which transfers every existential truth from the true graph pair \((r_0,r_1)\) to the false pair \((s_0,r_1)\).
